name: WMI Event Consumer Persistence
notes:
This rule detects WMI command line event consumers.

Covered from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Thomas Patzke
SIGMA Title: WMI Persistence - Command Line Event Consumer
SIGMA Description: Detects WMI command line event consumers


Confidence: LOW

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event QID is one of the following (5001844) Image loaded
when the event matches Process Name (custom) is any of WmiPrvSE.exe
when the event matches Filename (custom) is any of wbemcons.dll
TD_list: APPLY WMI Event Consumer Persistence on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event QID is one of the following (5001844) Image loaded
AND when the event matches Process Name (custom) is any of WmiPrvSE.exe
AND when the event matches Filename (custom) is any of wbemcons.dll
RED_list: Event name: WMI Event Consumer Persistence
Event description: Detects WMI command line event consumers
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
